clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Provide evidence-based recommendation for potassium-based salt substitutes to prevent or treat elevated BP and hypertension
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Adults with or without hypertension who use salt in food preparation or flavoring at home
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Recommend potassium-based salt substitutes (25-30% KCl) with monitoring exceptions

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for salt substitute recommendation
Screen for CKD and potassium-reducing medications
List potassium-reducing drugs for provider review

validationRules: Product must contain 25-30% KCl to be recommended
Products without KCl have unknown effects
CKD or potassium-reducing drugs require monitoring
Serum potassium monitoring indicated for at-risk patients

rules: Determine if patient has CKD or takes potassium-reducing drugs
If yes → require serum potassium monitoring
Verify salt substitute contains 25-30% potassium chloride
If product lacks KCl → effect is unknown (not recommended)
Classify based on patient profile and product composition

categories.name: Recommended
categories.condition: No CKD, no potassium-reducing drugs, home salt preparation
categories.action: Can be useful to prevent/treat elevated BP

categories.name: Unknown
categories.action: Refer to product label for KCl content

category.description: e.g., 'COR 2a | LOE A - Moderate Strength Recommendation'

requiresMonitoring.description: True if CKD or potassium-reducing drugs present

symptomsToAsk: Signs of hyperkalemia (muscle weakness, fatigue, palpitations, paresthesias) if on potassium-reducing drugs

escalationLogic.ifAnySymptomPresent: Check serum potassium immediately, consider discontinuing salt substitute

appDisplay.behavior: Proceed with caution for at-risk patients

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: For CKD or patients on potassium-reducing drugs: order baseline and periodic serum potassium levels
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Check serum potassium within 2-4 weeks of starting salt substitute, then every 3-6 months
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Monitor BP response to salt substitute after 4-8 weeks
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag if serum potassium >5.0 mEq/L or patient develops hyperkalemia symptoms
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log salt substitute recommendation, product verification, and monitoring schedule
